items.title: [正面] First off, I'm NOT a huge fan of card deck builder...
items.summary:
First off, I'm NOT a huge fan of card deck builder type of games, not my kind of niche and I'm not their targeted audience. The first time I saw Morimens, my first impression would be is their art is completely my cup of tea, and I'm a huuuge sucker for Lovecraftian worlds and styles. I thought, maybe I can give it a shot. Because [h2]the ART[/h2] 

To my surprise, Morimens is my first card deck builder game and I thoroughly enjoyed it.

It's your standard gacha game, but with cards, and [h2]BEAUTIFUL ART![/h2], and very good storyline plots (I cried a little going thru their story), and with generous devs sometimes giving you a lot of free silver core resources (a currency that you can usually topup using real money like how other gacha games work) through events, missions, collections and even as compensation throughout the game. And this game does not heavily depend on meta build, since pvp are not exactly their main focus or the strong suit. It heavily focuses on their main story, and other game modes which is basically straight up 'pve'. And you can explore a lot of cool builds and team comp using a lot of character combination, and there's no wrong answer to what is the 'best' comp in this game! Everything goes!

I dare say that this is one of the very generous gacha games (and I compare this with OTHER gacha games, so understand the standard I'm putting here) that is very F2P-friendly to users, and I highly, HIGHLY recommend if you like : 

- card games, 
- Lovecraftian art styles and storytelling, 
- generous rare resources, (at early game, so save wisely)
- and flexible and creative team comps that has no true META

because this game will not disappoint you!

And if you like a character, whether because you like how they look or you think that they are OP, whatever it is, just go for it! All of the characters are naturally good in their own way, [b]especially[/b] when you learn how to use them to their full potential! I would mostly go for the characters that I like visually before anything, because you will enjoy the game more and eventually you'll figure out how to make good use of your favorite character in the long-run once you get more characters.

Now,

The cons.

The cons so far I get from this game is that the pvp is a little bit...eh. Not much going on unless you want to get some tickets for the pfp and chat emotes (which no one uses it btw), and the matchmaking pretty much suck, but I'd say it's okay-ish for me since I'm not a die-hard pvp-er. And once you get to the mid game, the rarer resources are scarcer to get, so it's harder to get more compared to early game (obviously), which is why frugal saving and planning is important when you want to pull a certain SSR character. 

My advice is to save it for the characters that you desperately want and really like, don't go for everyone unless you go P2W (which like any other gacha games, I highly advise don't use it! You never know if the game is gonna shut down or something in the future). Please be careful and cautious when it comes to pulling characters because I noticed a review and agreed that this game is using the FOMO method through the 2.5 Anniversary Event to get people to use real money to get the rarer characters, keep in mind I just started playing this game very recently to make any more comments or comparisons on the current version and the older versions, but that one seems very obvious. So the bad reviews actually hold water on their concerns and I'm glad they mention it for everyone to know (I almost fell victim to the trap, ngl). 

Another issue about the game is that they practically make a lot of game modes, and some of them are basically repetitive and a hassle except the little difference for them is the materials they give you for each mode. You shared ranking rewards from different game modes (like, they only give rewards to the highest ranking out of 3 different game modes they offered), and some mode where you collect materials to upgrade your characters' skills are limited to one play per week (like, that's kinda little...) which makes you have to wait for another week just to play that mode again to get very little materials. So, it is straight up very grindy and time consuming, both on the waiting game and the minutes per run. I sometimes ended up just played a little and exit the game after I got my dailys (which is good I think...?)

If you are convinced and want to play this game, then I would like to give a heads-up and a little tip to newbies here. The game will give you one infinite reroll at the start of the game, so you can choose one SSR character at the start of your journey. I recommend getting one of these characters as starters :-

- Caecus (very well-balanced and straightforward)
- Nautila (VERY good tank)
- Goliath (good DPS/Tank)

 The rest of the characters are still good, but the mentioned characters above will help A LOT on going thru your story chapters when you are starting the game. But if you are more to a late-game investor, then I highly recommend getting starters like :-

- Winkle (ultra tank)
- Jenkin  (crazy DMG dealer)
- Faint  (your baby-eating death resisting savior)

These characters need a lot of time to build up and getting used to, because they work best on the system of the category they are in. The character are divided into 4 categories, from most beginner-friendly to the hardest :

- Aequor (Tentacle team)
-  Chaos (Supports the other teams)
- Caro (Baby-eating team)
- Ultra (Who-starts-first-gets-a-special-turn team)

To make it easy, I recommend picking characters from the Aequor and Chaos team (you'll know which one when u see it). Caro teams are fun once you start getting the hang of the game, and I can't say about Ultra that much since rn I rarely use them at my current knowledge level, since I just started playing less than a month ago, but I can say is that Ultra uses a lot of points in a single turn, because most of their cards cost a lot, but they give a lot of damage and status effects if you know how to use them. And after 5 (or 6?) turns, you will automatically get an extra turn which gives you an advantage by taking the enemy's turn. 

But however you choose, you don't get to worry about not getting these characters, because they are [b]veeery[/b] easy to get in the standard banner, soon enough you will eventually get almost all of them!

[h1]FINAL RECOMMENDATION[/h1]

So my final words of recommendation; I came for [h2]the ART[/h2], and I stayed for [b]almost everything[/b]. The game is enjoyable at best if you play it at your own pace and avoid the gacha trap (an F2P player btw), you get to get everything that you need like everyone else (only slower paced) without spending a dime, and lastly, [h2]the ART![/h2]


And don't worry about the card dupe buff talks, you can still upgrade your characters without spending too much time and money on trying to pull blindly in the banner because they also offer another resource where you can buy (in-game currency) an item that can upgrade one level of the character of your own choice, and they will restock it after reaching a certain amount of days before they reset. But like I said, you can enjoy the game worry-free, but with a veeery slow pace and high money restrain.

[soon to be edited] If you are reading this before the 2.5 Anniversary Event, then I suggest you start taking the opportunity and play this game right now! Some of the rarer characters (which are limited time characters that can only be acquired through their banner reruns) will be opened for rolls in this huge event! And you will get a free Collaborated SSR character Saya in this event as well!

items.title: [正面] It's great! I wish the game a long life. Sadly, I...
items.summary:
It's great! I wish the game a long life. Sadly, I play too many gachas already so I just checked this one out. I will follow the story though. I'm waiting for the collab with Saya no Uta, because I love that visual novel.

10/10, please do check it out.

Do not feed off reading negative reviews or do not seek reasons not to play it. If it caught your eye, give it a try. It's a really good game, trust me.
items.review:
It's great! I wish the game a long life. Sadly, I play too many gachas already so I just checked this one out. I will follow the story though. I'm waiting for the collab with Saya no Uta, because I love that visual novel.

10/10, please do check it out.

Do not feed off reading negative reviews or do not seek reasons not to play it. If it caught your eye, give it a try. It's a really good game, trust me.

items.title: [正面] Steam版跟官網下載的帳號無法互通。角色、命輪抽本體，啟靈每月在商店換，都能到三啟。每十等敵人數值...
items.summary: Steam版跟官網下載的帳號無法互通。角色、命輪抽本體，啟靈每月在商店換，都能到三啟。每十等敵人數值會大幅提升，復活道具每日回一，可存五個，同一場使用無上限。有四隊可嘗試打蝕災，再借好友角色帶一隊，慢慢拿等級突破的道具。一定要升技能，技能沒升滿手感、傷害差距很大，前期賢者之石(技能突破道具)需謹慎使用。
